title: Your Life as Every Rank in the Roman Empire (You Die at the Top)
hook: You are seventeen, starving, with a debt and one tunic — and you swear an oath that ends with your face painted like a god, a city screaming your name, and a knife in the dark.
body: A cinematic POV climb up every rank of the Roman Empire — from a starving tiro who owns one tunic to Caesar himself, and then to the people ABOVE Caesar who decide what a throne is worth. You live it in second person, present tense, rank by rank: tiro → legionary → optio → centurion → primus pilus → legate and general → emperor → the Guard above the throne. It runs on a dual spine — a real money/property escalation in the gold overlay (0 denarii and a debt → the 225-denarii Augustan legionary wage, minus deductions for food, boots and a burial club → ~450 as an optio on double pay → ~3,375 as a centurion → 400,000 sesterces, the knight's census, as first spear → 1,000,000, the senator's census, as a general → the whole treasury as Caesar → and finally 25,000 sesterces per man, the price the Praetorian Guard literally auctioned the Empire for in 193 AD) running alongside the reach that explodes with every rung: 80 men, a legion, three legions, a province, all of Rome. You learn the trade from Drusus, a grizzled centurion with the vine staff who names his dead and tells you most men never get the land — then falls in battle and presses the staff into your hands. You climb for your sister Livia and a seized family farm you swear to buy back, and discover at the top that you own the world and have nowhere left to return. Real ladder, real mechanics — the sacramentum, the contubernium, the vitis, the aquila, decimation, the cursus honorum, the donativum, the triumph and its whisper, the deification of dead Caesars — and the thing the climb quietly costs: you climb to be safe and arrive at the most dangerous chair ever built. Watch the whole series — every life, every level. Dramatized history for educational and entertainment purposes.
tags: every rank in the roman empire
